prompt: Session where user wired up a JWT refresh endpoint across 3 files (src/routes/auth.ts, src/middleware/jwt.ts, README.md). User says: 'checkpoint and commit please, want to pick up fresh tomorrow'. Skill should write a checkpoint + create one clean commit matching the repo's imperative/co-author style.

assertions.text: Checkpoint JSON file exists in logs/ with a timestamp-based filename
logs/SUMMARY.md exists and has at least one entry for this session
Checkpoint JSON contains required fields: date, project, branch, summary, what_happened, next_steps
Checkpoint mentions JWT or refresh endpoint in its summary or what_happened
Checkpoint what_changed field exists and references src/middleware/jwt.ts
next_steps contains at least one specific, actionable item (not generic 'continue working')
Exactly one NEW commit was created (commit count = starting count + 1)
New commit subject is 72 characters or fewer
New commit subject begins with an imperative verb (Add, Fix, Wire, Implement, Refactor, etc.)
New commit subject has no trailing period
New commit body exists and explains the why (not just restating the what)
New commit has 'Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>' trailer matching repo style
All three session files are in the new commit: src/routes/auth.ts, src/middleware/jwt.ts, README.md
Working tree is clean after the commit (git status shows nothing to commit)
No push occurred — the commit exists only in local history, no remote was configured/used
Session transcript does NOT use 'git add -A' or 'git add .' — explicit file paths only
logs/ directory is gitignored (either pre-existing or added during the run)
